title: antidesma pedicellare
intro: Antidesma pedicellare, also known as the fire tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Phyllanthaceae. It is native to Southeast Asia and is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The fire tree is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues, due to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of Antidesma pedicellare are often used to create infusions, decoctions, and tinctures for medicinal purposes.
